ADDED TO LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR Petition For Writ Of Mandate And Immediate Stay
Off Calendar Feb-17-2004 Continued To Mar-29-04 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Robert H. Tyler, Esq., Benjamin W. Bull, Esq. and Dale M. Schowengerdt, Esq., Alliance Defense Fund (909) 699-5050, for Petitioner and Moving Party Proposition 22 Legal Defense And Education Fund
Terry L. Thompson, Esq. (925) 855-1507 for Petitioner and Moving Party Proposition 22 Legal Defense And Education Fund
Dennis J. Herrera, City Attorney, Therese M. Stewart, Chief Deputy City Attorney, Joanne Hoeper, Chief Trial Deputy, Sherry Kaiser, Deputy City Attorney, Wayne K. Snodgrass, Deputy City Attorney, K. Scott Dickey, Deputy City Attorney (415) 554-3800 for Respondent City and County of San Francisco
Bobbie J. Wilson, Esq. and Pamela K. Fulmer, Esq., Howard Rice Nemerovski Canady Falk & Rabkin (415) 434-1600 for Respondent City and County of San Francisco
Waukeen Q. McCoy, Esq. (415) 675-7705 for Intervenor Bernan, et al
Shannon Minter, Esq. and Courtney Joslin, Esq., National Center For Lesbian Rights (415) 392-6257 for Intervenor Martin, et al
Tamara Lange, Esq., American Civil Liberties Union (415) 293-6361, for Intervenor Martin, et al
Victor Marquez, Esq., The Marquez Law Group LLP (415) 773-0242, Amicus Brief
RULING:
Court discloses possible conflict. Amicus brief may be filed by The Marquez Law Group LLP; order signed. Ex Parte Application For Leave To Intervene filed by Martin, et al is GRANTED; no opposition.A R G U E D; PETITIONER PROPOSITION 22 LEGAL DEFENSE AND EDUCATION FUND'S Petition For Writ Of Mandate And Immediate Stay: The Alternative Writ of Mandate shall issue to the Respondents City and County of San Francisco, Gavin Newsom and Nancy Alfaro who shall immediately, upon receipt, cease and desist from issuing marriage licenses or performing marriage ceremonies or appear on 03/29/04 at 1:00 PM to Show Cause why they have not done so. No stay is entered in this matter. Attorney Tyler to prepare form of order with approval as to form. (301/JLW) Reporter: C.Paxton 1558

ADDED TO LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR Petition For Writ Of Mandate And Immediate Stay
Off Calendar Feb-13-2004 Continued To Feb-17-04 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Terry L. Thompson, Esq. (925) 855-1507, for Petitioner Proposition 22 Legal Defense and Education Fund
Robert M. Tyler, Esq., Alliance Defense Fund, (909) 699-5050 for Petitioner Proposition 22 Legal Defense and Education Fund
Dennis J. Herrera, City Attorney, (415) 554-4700 for Respondent City and County of San Francisco
Therese M. Stewart, Chief Deputy City Attorney, (415) 554-4700 for Respondent City and County of San Francisco
Clyde J. Wadsworth, Esq., Steefel, Levitt & Weiss (415) 788-0900, for Intervenors Martin, Lyon, McShane, D'Amario, Conner, Smith and Chandler
Tamara Lange, Esq. (415) 293-6361 for Respondents
RULING:
A R G U E D; Petition For Writ Of Mandate And Immediate Stay: The Court finds, pursuant to 379 CCP, that insufficient notice was given to Respondent. The matter is CONTINUED to 02/17/04 at 2:00 PM in Dept. 301. Counsel are directed to serve their briefs no later than 12:00 Noon on Monday, 02/16/04. (301/JLW/EH) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
